title: Virtual Patients Using Large Language Models: Scalable, Contextualized Simulation of Clinician-Patient Dialogue With Feedback
citation: Cook DA, Overgaard J, Pankratz VS, Del Fiol G, Aakre CA. Virtual Patients Using Large Language Models: Scalable, Contextualized Simulation of Clinician-Patient Dialogue With Feedback. J Med Internet Res. 2025;27:e68486. doi:10.2196/68486.
permitted_uses: Scholarly support for LLM-powered virtual patient dialogue, patient preference representation, and feedback workflows.
claims_supported: The study used LLM-powered virtual patients to generate clinician-patient dialogues and feedback.
The study included outpatient chronic cough diagnosis and diabetes management topics.
The authors concluded that LLM-powered virtual patients can simulate patient-clinician dialogues and provide personalized feedback.
claims_not_supported: That LLM feedback is sufficient for high-stakes scoring.
That OpenClinXR's XR embodiment or voice system is validated.
